title: Human-to-Robot Skill Transfer using LfD: A Systematic Review
author: Francisco Neves

steps.instruction: This protocol defines the methodological framework for a systematic literature review focused on Learning from Demonstration (LfD) methods and their applications in robotic manipulation. The study aims to analyze the evolution, methodological trends, teaching modalities, and theoretical challenges associated with human-to-robot skill transfer in dynamic industrial environments. Publications were retrieved from major scientific databases and analyzed through a structured review process guided by the PRISMA 2020 framework. The research examines core learning approaches, including Behavior Cloning and Inverse Reinforcement Learning, together with teaching modalities such as kinesthetic guiding and teleoperation. Additionally, the study investigates fundamental challenges including the correspondence problem and policy generalization, providing a comprehensive overview of the current state and future directions of adaptable robotic manipulation systems.

Population Stationary (fixed-base) robotic manipulators. Intervention LfD algorithms. Comparison Traditional hard-coded programming; RL without prior demonstrations; Hybrid LfD and RL architectures; Cross-modality LfD comparisons. Outcome Learning/sample efficiency and overhead; Task success and trajectory accuracy; Generalization to novel parameters. Context Industrial applications of robot learning, focusing on LfD techniques for manipulation.

Which LfD methods are applied to robotic manipulators? Which demonstration modalities and sensors are most common? Which evaluation metrics and benchmarks are used for comparability? What are the main technical limitations and open research directions?

Inclusion Criteria 2020–2026 Language English full-text Venue Peer-reviewed (IROS, ICRA, CVPR, CoRL) Method LfD, IL, or PbD focus Hardware Robotic manipulators Validation Empirical manipulation tasks Exclusion Criteria Prior to 2020 Non-English Non-peer-reviewed (books, workshops) No demonstration-based learning Non-manipulation platforms Purely theoretical/ No experiments

Is the study from a peer-reviewed, top-tier venue? Is the experimental setup clearly described? Are the robot manipulators well-specified? Is the LfD method clearly defined (algorithm, modality)? Are the demonstrations clearly described? Is a baseline comparison (e.g., RL, traditional) included? Are performance outcomes reported? Are efficiency outcomes (e.g., sample/time) reported? Are generalization or robustness outcomes reported? Is there a sufficient number of trials or demonstrations? Is the study reproducibility documented? Are technical limitations explicitly acknowledged? Scoring criteria were defined as follows: 0 indicating no compliance, 0.5 indicating partial compliance, and 1 indicating full compliance.

General Query ("learn* from demonstration" OR "learn* by demonstration" OR "imitation learning") AND (manipulat* OR "robot arm") AND PublicationYear >= 2020 AND PublicationYear 2019 AND PUBYEAR 2019 AND PUBYEAR 2019 AND PUBYEAR < 2026 IEEE Xplore (("Document Title":learn* AND ("Document Title":demonstration OR "Document Title":imitation) AND ("Document Title":manipulat* OR "Document Title":"robot arm")))

- The PRISMA 2020 methodology was followed for systematic selection and using the framework Parsifal. - Articles were filtered using keyword queries, then screened manually for topical relevance. - Duplicates, non-relevant, and retracted records were excluded. - The final selection is illustrated bellow.
